skill.name: production-code-audit

skill.summary: Autonomously deep-scan entire codebase line-by-line, understand architecture and patterns, then syst...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 53 static findings are false positives. External command detections are markdown code examples. Network URLs are documentation links. Environment access and cryptographic references describe security fixes to apply to user codebases, not vulnerabilities in this skill itself. This is a documentation skill that explains how to audit and fix production code.

content.user_title: Audit and Improve Production Code Quality
content.value_statement: Codebases often have hidden security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and architectural problems that are missed during development. This skill autonomously scans entire codebases, identifies all issues, and automatically transforms code to production-grade enterprise quality.
actual_capabilities: Autonomously scan and analyze entire codebases line-by-line
Identify security vulnerabilities including SQL injection, XSS, and hardcoded secrets
Detect performance issues such as N+1 queries, missing indexes, and inefficient algorithms
Automatically fix identified issues with production-ready solutions
Refactor architecture and break up god classes into focused services
Add production infrastructure including logging, monitoring, and health checks
limitations: Does not execute fixed code - provides instructions for fixes only
Cannot guarantee all edge cases are covered in auto-generated tests
May require human review for complex architectural changes
Does not modify external dependencies or infrastructure configuration

use_cases.title: Pre-deployment Code Review
use_cases.description: Scan and fix entire codebase before production deployment to meet enterprise standards
use_cases.target_user: Development teams preparing for production release

use_cases.title: Security Hardening
use_cases.description: Identify and fix all security vulnerabilities including injection attacks, weak cryptography, and exposed secrets

use_cases.title: Legacy Code Modernization
use_cases.description: Transform legacy codebases into modern, maintainable, production-ready applications
use_cases.target_user: Teams maintaining older codebases needing modernization

prompt_templates.title: Basic Production Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the production-code-audit skill to scan this codebase and make it production-ready. Find all issues across security, performance, architecture, and quality, then fix them automatically.

prompt_templates.title: Security Focus
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the production-code-audit skill to audit this codebase for security vulnerabilities. Find SQL injection, XSS, hardcoded secrets, weak authentication, and other security issues, then fix them.

prompt_templates.title: Performance Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the production-code-audit skill to optimize this codebase for performance. Find N+1 queries, missing indexes, inefficient algorithms, and other performance bottlenecks, then fix them.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Transformation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the production-code-audit skill to perform a complete production-grade transformation. Scan all files, identify all issues across every category, fix everything automatically, add production infrastructure, and verify with tests.

output: Phase 2: Scanning for Issues - Found 127 issues (8 critical, 23 high, 41 medium, 55 low)
Phase 3: Fixing Issues Automatically - All 127 issues fixed
Phase 4: Adding Production Infrastructure - Logging, monitoring, health checks added
Phase 5: Verification - All tests passing, 87% coverage, 0 vulnerabilities
Security Scan Complete - Found 15 security issues
Fixed: SQL injection, hardcoded secrets, missing authentication, weak password hashing, input validation
Performance Analysis: 3.2s API response time → 180ms (94% improvement)
Optimizations: N+1 queries fixed, Redis caching added, indexes created

best_practices: Run the audit before any production deployment to catch issues early
Review the generated report to understand what was fixed and why
Run tests after audit to verify fixes did not break existing functionality
Schedule regular quarterly audits to maintain code quality over time
anti_patterns: Do not ignore critical and high-priority findings - they represent real risks
Do not skip the verification step - always run tests after making changes
Do not rely solely on auto-generated tests - add manual test coverage for complex logic

faq.question: What does this skill actually do?
faq.answer: This skill scans your entire codebase, identifies issues across security, performance, architecture, and quality, then automatically fixes them to meet production standards.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to use on production code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill reads and analyzes code, then provides fixes. It does not execute arbitrary code or make external network requests. Always backup your code before running.

faq.question: What security issues can it find and fix?
faq.answer: It detects SQL injection, XSS, hardcoded secrets, weak password hashing (MD5/SHA1), missing authentication/authorization, input validation gaps, and insecure dependencies.

faq.question: Does it work with all programming languages?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes code patterns and can work across many languages. Best results are seen with JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, and Go codebases.

faq.question: How long does a complete audit take?
faq.answer: Audit time depends on codebase size. A typical 100-file project takes 2-5 minutes. Larger codebases may take longer but the skill works autonomously.

faq.question: What happens if the skill breaks my code?
faq.answer: The skill provides fixes but does not execute them automatically. You can review each change before applying. Always run tests after to verify everything works correctly.
